---
title: Transcriptional adaptation mediated by mRNA decay
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
author: "Zhenguo Zhang"
format:
    gfm:
        toc: false
        code-fold: true
---

## Overview 📚

**Transcriptional adaptation** is a cellular mechanism where cells upregulate related genes in response to genetic perturbations, effectively masking deleterious phenotypes of mutations.

## Recent Discovery 🔬

El-Brolosy et al. (2026) revealed a novel mechanism in *Science*: degraded mRNAs mediate transcriptional adaptation by binding to antisense transcripts of related genes,  which activates transcription of compensatory genes 🧬. This process is guided by the protein **ILF3**.

## Significance 💡

This discovery unveils a new regulatory dimension of mRNA decay and provides important insights into how cells adapt to genetic perturbations.

**Reference:** https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/science.aea1272
